title: Nagi Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Nagi Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Bihar

overview.short: Nagi Bird Sanctuary is located in Jhajha Jamui district of south Bihar, near the Jharkhand border.
overview.description: Nagi Bird Sanctuary is located in Jhajha Jamui district of south Bihar, near the Jharkhand border. It was declared a bird sanctuary on 25 February 1984 as per Section 18 of the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972. The final notification under Section 26A of the act was issued on 4 September 2009. Thousands of migrating birds congregate in the reservoir during the winter season, especially from November to February.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nagi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nagi Bird Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nagi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport (146 km) and the nearest railway station is Dhanbad Junction (114 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nagi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nagi Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nagi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Nagi Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nagi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nakti Dam Wildlife Sanctuary, Jhajha.
